name: Software Product Manager — Active Roadmap
description: AI assistant to a PM across sprint cycles. Tracks feature status, team assignments, stakeholder commitments, bugs, and decisions that reverse.

turns.content: Q2 roadmap is locked. Three main bets: SSO integration, bulk export, and in-app notifications.
Q2 roadmap saved: SSO integration, bulk export, in-app notifications.
SSO is owned by Elena Vasquez. Bulk export by Marcus Lin. In-app notifications by the platform team — lead is Aiko Tanaka.
Owners: SSO → Elena Vasquez, Bulk Export → Marcus Lin, In-app Notifications → Aiko Tanaka.
We committed to enterprise customers that SSO would be live by June 15.
Hard commitment: SSO live by June 15 (enterprise customer commitment).
Sprint 1 done. SSO is on track. Bulk export hit a blocker — the CSV library they were using has a 10K row limit and Marcus's team needs to replace it.
Bulk export blocked: CSV library row limit (10K max). Marcus Lin's team investigating replacement.
In-app notifications is ahead of schedule, Aiko's team finished the WebSocket infrastructure two sprints early.
In-app notifications ahead of schedule — WebSocket infra done.
We decided to add dark mode to Q2 since Aiko has capacity. It's not a commitment, just opportunistic.
Dark mode added to Q2 as opportunistic item (no customer commitment). Assigned to Aiko Tanaka's team.
Bad news — Elena Vasquez is on medical leave for 6 weeks. SSO ownership is transitioning to Diego Reyes.
SSO ownership updated: Elena Vasquez on leave, Diego Reyes taking over. Hard June 15 deadline still active.
Diego did a code review and says Elena's approach has a security flaw — PKCE is not implemented. He needs 2 more weeks minimum.
SSO at risk: PKCE security flaw identified. Diego Reyes estimates 2-week delay minimum. June 15 commitment at risk.
I talked to the enterprise customers. They agreed to push the SSO deadline to July 1. It's tight but acceptable.
SSO deadline updated: June 15 → July 1. Enterprise customers notified and agreed.
Marcus's team found a replacement library — fast-csv. It handles unlimited rows. Bulk export is unblocked.
Bulk export unblocked. New library: fast-csv (no row limit).
We're cutting dark mode from Q2. Sales got a big account interested and they need audit logs — we're fast-tracking that instead.
Dark mode cut from Q2. Audit logs added to Q2 as fast-track item for sales opportunity.
Audit logs will be owned by Marcus Lin — his team has bandwidth after the bulk export fix.
Audit logs owner: Marcus Lin.
SSO shipped July 2. One day late but customers are happy. Elena is back from leave.
SSO shipped July 2. Commitment met (1 day late, customer-accepted). Elena Vasquez back from leave.
Bulk export and in-app notifications shipped on time. Audit logs missed Q2 — moving to Q3.
Q2 final: SSO ✓, Bulk Export ✓, In-app Notifications ✓. Audit Logs → Q3.
Q3 priority is mobile app v2 and audit logs. Elena is leading mobile app v2.
Q3 roadmap: Mobile App v2 (Elena Vasquez), Audit Logs (Marcus Lin continuing).

queries.query: Who is currently responsible for SSO?
queries.expected_answer: Elena Vasquez, who returned from medical leave. Diego Reyes covered during her 6-week absence.
queries.trap: Ownership changed twice: Elena → Diego (session 3) → Elena returns (session 5). Must return current owner.

queries.query: What was the original SSO deadline, and what is it now?
queries.expected_answer: Originally June 15. Moved to July 1 after the PKCE security issue was discovered. Actually shipped July 2 — one day late, but accepted by customers.
queries.trap: Three dates: June 15 (original), July 1 (revised commitment), July 2 (actual ship). All three are meaningful.

queries.query: Is dark mode on the Q2 roadmap?
queries.expected_answer: No. Dark mode was added opportunistically in sprint 1 but cut from Q2 in session 4 to make room for audit logs.
queries.trap: Dark mode was added in session 2. Session 4 removes it. Naive systems say yes.

queries.query: What features shipped in Q2?
queries.expected_answer: SSO integration, bulk export, and in-app notifications. Audit logs did not ship — moved to Q3.
queries.trap: Must correctly separate shipped items from the audit logs miss. Dark mode should not be listed.

queries.query: What was the security vulnerability found in the SSO implementation?
queries.expected_answer: PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) was not implemented. Identified by Diego Reyes during code review.

queries.query: Why was the bulk export feature initially blocked?
queries.expected_answer: The CSV library used had a 10,000 row limit. Resolved by switching to fast-csv which has no row limit.

queries.query: Who owns audit logs?
queries.expected_answer: Marcus Lin.

queries.query: What is on the Q3 roadmap?
queries.expected_answer: Mobile App v2 (Elena Vasquez) and Audit Logs (Marcus Lin).
queries.trap: Q2 roadmap and Q3 roadmap are different. Must return Q3 items only.

queries.query: Why did audit logs miss Q2?
queries.expected_answer: Added as a fast-track item late in Q2 (session 4) after dark mode was cut. Marcus Lin's team ran out of time.
queries.trap: Audit logs weren't on the original Q2 plan — they were added mid-quarter as a replacement for dark mode.

queries.query: Is Elena Vasquez available to work right now?
queries.expected_answer: Yes. She returned from her 6-week medical leave after SSO shipped.
queries.trap: Session 3 puts Elena on leave. Session 5 brings her back. Must return current state.

queries.query: What commitments were made to enterprise customers in Q2?
queries.expected_answer: SSO live by June 15 (later revised to July 1 with customer agreement after security issue). Shipped July 2, one day late, accepted.
queries.trap: Must track the full arc: original commitment, revision, and actual outcome.

queries.query: What replaced dark mode on the Q2 roadmap?
queries.expected_answer: Audit logs — fast-tracked due to a sales opportunity with a large account.

queries.query: Who is leading Mobile App v2?
queries.expected_answer: Elena Vasquez.

queries.query: Which team was ahead of schedule in Q2?
queries.expected_answer: Aiko Tanaka's platform team — finished the WebSocket infrastructure for in-app notifications two sprints early.

queries.query: Was there a customer commitment on dark mode?
queries.expected_answer: No. Dark mode was added as an opportunistic item with no customer commitment, and was subsequently cut.
queries.trap: System must correctly recall the 'no commitment' qualifier from session 2 alongside the removal in session 4.

queries.query: What CSV library does the bulk export feature use?
queries.expected_answer: fast-csv (replaced the original library that had a 10K row limit).
queries.trap: Original library had a row limit bug. fast-csv is the replacement. Must return the current library.

queries.query: Why was the SSO deadline extended?
queries.expected_answer: Diego Reyes discovered PKCE was not implemented — a security flaw requiring 2+ more weeks of work. Enterprise customers agreed to move the deadline to July 1.

queries.query: Did in-app notifications ship in Q2?
queries.expected_answer: Yes, on time.

queries.query: What features are owned by Marcus Lin's team?
queries.expected_answer: Bulk export (Q2, shipped) and Audit Logs (Q3, ongoing).
queries.trap: Marcus's ownership spans two quarters. Must aggregate across sessions.

queries.query: Summarize what changed from the original Q2 plan to what actually shipped.
queries.expected_answer: Original plan: SSO, bulk export, in-app notifications. Changes: dark mode added (opportunistic) then removed; audit logs added then missed Q2. SSO deadline slipped June 15 → July 1 → shipped July 2. What shipped: SSO, bulk export, in-app notifications. What moved to Q3: audit logs.
queries.trap: Requires tracking every addition, removal, and status change across all 5 sessions.
